meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve 'No Project Ideas'
content.seo_description: Stop guessing what to build. Learn how to implement advanced analytics to identify market gaps and validate your next SaaS venture using Antigravity and Claude.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing abstract ideas. Use event-based analytics to track user friction in existing workflows, revealing where your product can provide actual value. By integrating automated tracking, you transform 'no ideas' into data-backed opportunities, ensuring you build software that solves real pain points and generates recurring revenue from day one.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank repository, waiting for a lightning bolt of inspiration that never comes. Most founders fall into the trap of 'solution-searching,' burning months on features nobody asked for. The graveyard of SaaS is filled with products that were technically perfect but commercially irrelevant.

The secret isn't a better brainstorming session; it's data-driven empathy. By deploying lightweight analytics early, you stop inventing problems and start discovering them. We’re moving beyond vanity metrics to capture the high-intent signals that define a viable business model.

steps.title: Instrument High-Intent Events
steps.description: Identify where users struggle in your current MVP or competitor workflows. We need to track specific 'intent' events to see where users get stuck.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for event tracking that captures user friction points, specifically focusing on drop-off rates during critical path actions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'instrument analytics events in src/hooks/useTracking.ts'

steps.title: Configure Antigravity Data Pipeline
steps.description: Set up the ingestion layer so that every click is mapped to a potential feature request or pain point.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the Antigravity pipeline to aggregate user interaction data and export it into a structured format for pattern analysis.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add antigravity-sdk && antigravity init --project-type analytics

steps.title: Automate Pattern Detection
steps.description: Use Claude Code to parse your analytics logs and identify recurring 'no-solution' behaviors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the last 30 days of event logs to identify clusters of user frustration that correlate with missing functionality.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'analyze logs and identify top 3 user friction clusters'

steps.title: Validate Market Demand with Synthetic Users
steps.description: Before writing production code, use AI to simulate user feedback on your proposed solution.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a lean validation script that tests the proposed feature set against the identified user pain points.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate validation logic for identified pain points'

steps.title: Iterate for Revenue Velocity
steps.description: Refine the analytics feedback loop to ensure your new features directly influence conversion metrics.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a dashboard view in Antigravity that correlates specific feature usage with user upgrade conversion rates.

pro_tips: Never track everything; track only the events that correlate with a user reaching their 'Aha!' moment.
Use session replays alongside event analytics to see the human emotion behind the data drop-off.
Treat your analytics schema like your database schema; technical debt here is costly to fix later.
If you can't find a problem worth paying for in your data, pivot before you write the first line of code.
Set up automated alerts for 'feature-request-signals'—when 5+ users hit a dead-end button, that's your next product.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, spend 48 hours observing real user behavior. Map the top three friction points, draft a one-page PRD, and use Claude Code to scaffold your MVP in under four hours.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start building for scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
